feedback.text: I'd love if the EU would take Videogames into consideration for the digital fairness act. The "Stop Killing Games" movement has already started to collect signatures and has reached their goal of 1 million signatures with a bonus of 400.000 extra signatures as a safety net on top. Stop Killing Games: https://eci.ec.europa.eu/045/public/#/screen/home There have already been numerous games to which consumers that have paid money to own and access them have lost the access because the developers revoked it due to the game ending its lifecycle. The Game "The Crew" is one example. Ubisoft shut its servers down without providing a way for the community to host their own servers to be able to continue playing the game. The game could even be played in Singleplayer but it had a forced internet connection. Now that the servers are down, people cannot even play it in singleplayer. They effectively lost all their rights to the product they bought because Ubisoft centered the design of their game around forced internet connections even though the mechanics to play it in singleplayer are there. And that is just one of many examples out there. Please look into this and think of more ways to protect us consumers, these corporations are taking our money and putting us into a corner. Best regards, Marc Neumann
